title: polypodium papuense
intro: Polypodium papuense, also known as Papuan polypody, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of New Guinea. It is an evergreen epiphyte that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Polypodium papuense include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Polypodium papuense is often used in the form of tea, decoctions, or infusions, and its leaves can also be used to make a poultice for topical application.
